waves.name: Atlas Compatibility + Surface Contract
waves.goal: Make Sentinel Atlas-compatible by defining the adapter contract, normalizing config vocabulary, and preserving a clean migration path from the current flat catalog model.

checkpoints.name: Config and type model are honest
checkpoints.description: Platform keys, catalog types, and surface/scenario vocabulary line up with real ecosystem usage and Atlas expectations.

checkpoints.name: Migration contract exists
checkpoints.description: Sentinel can explain how Atlas manifests, legacy catalog.screens configs, and product proof flows coexist during transition.

waves.name: Hierarchical Review + Migration
waves.goal: Upgrade Sentinel's dashboard, validation, and doctor paths so Atlas artifacts can be reviewed and validated without breaking existing product proof loops.

checkpoints.name: Hierarchical dashboard works
checkpoints.description: Sentinel can present surface path, scenario, and target hierarchies instead of only flat screen slugs.

checkpoints.name: Atlas validation path is real
checkpoints.description: Expected shots, diff, parity, doctor, and migration docs all support Atlas-backed proof without a flag-day break.

waves.name: Brand-Aware Atlas Diagnostics
waves.goal: Add a thin Sentinel follow-on wave that can inspect Brandie-backed Atlas review metadata, diagnose integration drift, and optionally expose that context in validation and review output without turning Sentinel into the primary review product.

checkpoints.name: Brand-aware fixtures exist
checkpoints.description: Sentinel has realistic Atlas fixtures showing how Brandie review metadata flows through the compatibility layer.

checkpoints.name: Doctor and validation classify review-metadata drift
checkpoints.description: Sentinel can tell the difference between missing screenshot proof and missing Brandie review context.

checkpoints.name: Optional review context stays visible but thin
checkpoints.description: Sentinel can surface Brandie review context where useful without competing with Atlas as the review UI.
